name: Bosgaurus Coffee
hem_note: A polished specialty stop with big-city energy and a strong sense of place along the riverfront side of Binh Thanh.
why_it_matters: One of the most recognizable modern specialty coffee names in Saigon, balancing accessibility with serious coffee standards.
best_for: espresso drinkers, meetings, introducing friends to specialty coffee
hat_note: Best matched to drinkers who want polished espresso service with a contemporary specialty profile rather than experimental funk.

name: The Workshop Coffee
address: 27 Ngo Duc Ke, Floor 2, Ben Nghe Ward
hem_note: A classic Saigon specialty address with a hidden upstairs feel that still reads like a destination for coffee people.
why_it_matters: The Workshop remains one of the city references for early specialty culture and careful hand-brew service.
best_for: pour-over drinkers, first-time cafe hopping in District 1, slow afternoons
hat_note: Strong pick if you value clarity, a slower service rhythm, and a menu that respects hand-brew coffee rather than just milk drinks.

name: XLIII Coffee (HCM)
address: 4 Luong Huu Khanh, Pham Ngu Lao Ward
hem_note: A severe, minimalist room that feels more like a tasting chamber than a casual neighborhood coffee stop.
why_it_matters: XLIII is one of the clearer examples of high-focus, presentation-driven specialty service in central Saigon.
best_for: serious espresso drinkers, coffee tourism, design-minded visitors
hat_note: More about exactness and ritual than comfort, making it ideal for people who want to compare coffees with as little distraction as possible.

name: Every Half Coffee Roasters Tu Xuong
address: 6E Tu Xuong, Vo Thi Sau Ward
hem_note: A lively roastery-led branch that still feels approachable, with enough movement and energy to make it feel like part workshop, part local hangout.
why_it_matters: Every Half has helped define the everyday face of specialty coffee in Saigon by scaling quality without losing coffee identity.
best_for: daily coffee runs, mixed groups, casual specialty drinkers
hat_note: A strong choice for people who want traceable Vietnamese coffee presented in a polished but still approachable way.

name: Soul Specialty Coffee
address: 386/11 Le Van Sy, Nhieu Loc Ward
hem_note: A more tucked-away space with a thoughtful Vietnamese identity, good for people who want the atmosphere to feel intentional rather than generic.
why_it_matters: Soul stands out for tying specialty coffee language to a distinctly Vietnamese brand voice instead of copying global cafe aesthetics.
best_for: quiet catchups, thoughtful solo visits, Vietnamese coffee curiosity
origin_focus: Vietnamese coffee with international support menu
beans: Vietnamese phin, espresso, and international beans
hat_note: Best for drinkers interested in how Vietnamese-origin coffee can sit comfortably inside a modern specialty framework.

name: 96B cafe & roastery
hem_note: A sharper, more coffee-first room where the energy leans toward experimentation, workshops, and people who actually want to talk beans.
why_it_matters: 96B is one of the more interesting references for Vietnam-focused specialty coffee, especially around Liberica, Excelsa, and education.
best_for: coffee geeks, workshops, trying Vietnam-grown varieties beyond arabica
hat_note: Particularly valuable if you want to explore the wider flavor range of Vietnamese-grown coffee rather than staying inside familiar imported-origin profiles.

name: Tonkin Specialty Coffee
address: 91 Ly Tu Trong, Ben Thanh Ward
hem_note: A central District 1 cafe that reads as friendly and distinctly Vietnamese, good for visitors who want local character without sacrificing comfort.
why_it_matters: Tonkin helps bridge specialty presentation with more familiar Vietnamese coffee storytelling and tourist-friendly access.
best_for: travelers, Vietnamese coffee introductions, central city breaks
hat_note: Less about hardcore competition-style coffee and more about presenting Vietnamese classics with enough polish to appeal to specialty-curious guests.

name: Amazing Specialty Coffee
address: 29 Luu Van Lang, Ben Thanh Ward
hem_note: A compact central hideout with a more curated, discovery-oriented mood that works well when you want a break from the louder tourist flow.
why_it_matters: Amazing adds another useful specialty stop near Ben Thanh with a broader educational framing around origins and exploration.
best_for: cafe hopping, central city breaks, origin exploration
roaster_type: specialty cafe with rotating coffee focus
hat_note: Useful for drinkers who like rotating coffees and want a central location that still feels aligned with specialty discovery rather than mass-market cafe culture.

name: 43 Factory Coffee
address: 422 Ngo Thi Sy, My An Ward
hem_note: Included as a reference record from the older dataset, but not shown on the current HCMC-only map.
why_it_matters: A strong regional specialty reference, though outside the current map focus.
hat_note: Kept mainly for continuity with the original dataset.
